content.text:
# Vibma Benchmark Challenge

You have Vibma MCP tools available in your environment. Use them directly — do NOT look at project files, start servers, or run shell commands to interact with Figma. The MCP server is already configured and connected.

Start by calling `join_channel` (channel: "vibma"), then `ping` to confirm the connection. After that, use the MCP tools for all Figma operations.

## Part 1: Design Tokens

You're starting with a blank Figma file. Build a complete design system foundation.

### Color Variables

Create a variable collection called **"Colors"** with two modes: **Light** and **Dark**.

Include at minimum:
- **Brand**: `brand/primary`, `brand/secondary` — your main brand colors
- **Accent scale**: `accent/100` through `accent/900` — a full tonal scale
- **Backgrounds**: `bg/primary`, `bg/secondary`, `bg/surface`, `bg/elevated`
- **Text**: `text/primary`, `text/secondary`, `text/muted`, `text/inverse`
- **Semantic**: `semantic/success`, `semantic/error`, `semantic/warning`, `semantic/info`
- **Border**: `border/default`, `border/subtle`

Each variable should have appropriate values for both Light and Dark modes.

### Text Styles

Create a type scale with distinct sizes and weights:
- **Display**: Large hero text
- **Heading**: H1, H2, H3
- **Body**: Large, Regular, Small
- **Caption**: Regular, Small
- **Label**: Medium, Small

Use a professional font family. Headings should be bold/semibold, body regular, captions/labels medium weight.

## Part 2: Design System Page

Create a new page called **"Benchmark"** and document your tokens:

- A **color palette** section showing all your color variables as labeled swatches, bound to the actual variables (not hardcoded hex)
- A **typography scale** section showing all your text styles with sample text
- Use auto-layout for layout. Name all layers semantically.

## Part 3: Components

Build the following 5 components on the Benchmark page. All must use your design tokens (color variables + text styles), auto-layout, and semantic naming.

### 1. Button
- Primary, secondary, and ghost variants
- Include a text label
- Proper padding and border radius

### 2. Navigation Bar
- Logo/brand text on the left
- Nav links in the middle (Features, Pricing, Testimonials)
- CTA button on the right ("Get Started")
- Full-width, horizontal layout

### 3. Feature Card
- Icon placeholder (square or circle frame)
- Feature title (heading style)
- Feature description (body style, 1-2 lines)
- Card background using surface color variable

### 4. Pricing Card
- Plan name (heading style)
- Price with period (e.g., "$29/mo")
- Feature list (3-5 bullet items)
- CTA button at the bottom
- Create 3 tiers: Free, Pro, Enterprise
- Highlight the Pro card as the recommended option (accent background or border)

### 5. Testimonial Card
- Avatar placeholder (circle)
- Quote text (body/italic style)
- Person name (label/bold style) and role/company (caption style)

## Part 4: SaaS Landing Page

Assemble your components into a **SaaS landing page** mockup on the Benchmark page:

1. **Nav bar** at the top
2. **Hero section**: Display heading, subtitle in body text, two buttons (primary "Get Started" + secondary "Learn More")
3. **Features section**: Section heading + 3 feature cards in a row
4. **Pricing section**: Section heading + 3 pricing cards side by side
5. **Testimonials section**: Section heading + 2-3 testimonial cards
6. **Footer**: Simple footer with brand name, copyright, and a few links

This should look like a real landing page — full width, proper spacing between sections, visual hierarchy.

## Part 5: Export

Export the Benchmark page as PNG.

## Scoring Criteria

- Created variable collection with Light/Dark modes and organized color scales
- Created distinct text styles with proper size/weight differentiation
- Color swatches bound to variables, not hardcoded
- All 5 components built with proper variants
- Components use design tokens throughout
- Landing page assembles components into a realistic, full-width layout
- Auto-layout and semantic naming throughout
- Final PNG exported
I’ll build this directly in Figma via Vibma tools only. First I’m joining the `vibma` channel and confirming the plugin connection with `ping`, then I’ll create the token system and Benchmark page.

text.text: Joined channel "vibma" on port 3055. Call `ping` now to verify the Figma plugin is connected.

content.text: Connection is live. Next I’m creating the `Benchmark` page plus a full `Colors` variable collection with Light/Dark modes, then I’ll populate all required color tokens and values.

I’ve finished the full Light/Dark color token set. I’m now creating the typography style system, then I’ll build the Benchmark page structure and token documentation sections with variable-bound swatches.
I’m building the token documentation now: a wrapped swatch grid where every swatch fill is directly bound to its color variable, followed by labeled typography specimens for every text style.

items.text: Connect your design tooling in minutes with clean APIs and prebuilt workflows.
Invite stakeholders, sync updates instantly, and keep everyone aligned across projects.
Protect data with SOC 2-ready controls, SSO, and robust permission management.
"Vibma helped our team ship a polished product page in two days."
"We replaced three disconnected tools with one reliable workflow."

content.text: I’m doing a final pass to ensure component instances read cleanly in the assembled layout, then I’ll export the full Benchmark page as a PNG and share the export reference.

Implemented end-to-end in Figma via Vibma MCP tools and exported the result.

- Connected successfully with `join_channel("vibma")` + `ping`.
- Created `Benchmark` page and built a full `Colors` variable collection with `Light` + `Dark` modes.
- Added all required color tokens:
  - `brand/*`, `accent/100..900`, `bg/*`, `text/*`, `semantic/*`, `border/*`
  - Values are set for both modes.
- Created full typography text styles:
  - `Display`, `Heading (H1/H2/H3)`, `Body (Large/Regular/Small)`, `Caption`, `Label`
  - Plus `Body/Regular Italic` for testimonial quotes.
- Built token documentation on the page:
  - Color swatch grid bound to real variables (not hardcoded)
  - Typography scale examples using text styles
- Built required components with auto-layout and semantic names:
  - `Button` as a variant set (`Primary`, `Secondary`, `Ghost`)
  - `Navigation Bar`
  - `Feature Card`
  - `Pricing Card` tiers (`Free`, `Pro`, `Enterprise`) with `Pro` highlighted
  - `Testimonial Card`
- Assembled full SaaS landing page:
  - Nav, Hero, Features, Pricing, Testimonials, Footer
  - Reused component instances and tokenized styles/colors.
- Exported the full `Benchmark` page as PNG (including a higher-resolution export at scale 2).

If you want, I can now do a strict accessibility/tokens lint pass (`wcag` + hardcoded-color rules) and patch any remaining issues automatically.

result:
I’ll build this directly in Figma via Vibma tools only. First I’m joining the `vibma` channel and confirming the plugin connection with `ping`, then I’ll create the token system and Benchmark page.Connection is live. Next I’m creating the `Benchmark` page plus a full `Colors` variable collection with Light/Dark modes, then I’ll populate all required color tokens and values.I’ve finished the full Light/Dark color token set. I’m now creating the typography style system, then I’ll build the Benchmark page structure and token documentation sections with variable-bound swatches.I’m building the token documentation now: a wrapped swatch grid where every swatch fill is directly bound to its color variable, followed by labeled typography specimens for every text style.Next I’m constructing all five component masters (including a true button variant set), then I’ll instantiate them into the landing page sections.Core component masters are now in place. I’m assembling the full landing page next (nav, hero, features, pricing, testimonials, footer) using these components and tokenized styles, then I’ll export PNG.I’m doing a final pass to ensure component instances read cleanly in the assembled layout, then I’ll export the full Benchmark page as a PNG and share the export reference.Implemented end-to-end in Figma via Vibma MCP tools and exported the result.

- Connected successfully with `join_channel("vibma")` + `ping`.
- Created `Benchmark` page and built a full `Colors` variable collection with `Light` + `Dark` modes.
- Added all required color tokens:
  - `brand/*`, `accent/100..900`, `bg/*`, `text/*`, `semantic/*`, `border/*`
  - Values are set for both modes.
- Created full typography text styles:
  - `Display`, `Heading (H1/H2/H3)`, `Body (Large/Regular/Small)`, `Caption`, `Label`
  - Plus `Body/Regular Italic` for testimonial quotes.
- Built token documentation on the page:
  - Color swatch grid bound to real variables (not hardcoded)
  - Typography scale examples using text styles
- Built required components with auto-layout and semantic names:
  - `Button` as a variant set (`Primary`, `Secondary`, `Ghost`)
  - `Navigation Bar`
  - `Feature Card`
  - `Pricing Card` tiers (`Free`, `Pro`, `Enterprise`) with `Pro` highlighted
  - `Testimonial Card`
- Assembled full SaaS landing page:
  - Nav, Hero, Features, Pricing, Testimonials, Footer
  - Reused component instances and tokenized styles/colors.
- Exported the full `Benchmark` page as PNG (including a higher-resolution export at scale 2).

If you want, I can now do a strict accessibility/tokens lint pass (`wcag` + hardcoded-color rules) and patch any remaining issues automatically.
